violations.text: The inside of the chest freezer lid was observed cracked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerator and milk cooler gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (components of principal's popcorn machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings near the tray return area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025
The following was observed in poor repair: -gaskets on small milk cooler -lid to chest freezer. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The faucet at the prep line sink was observed with a leak. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the tray return area (salad bar, popcorn machine, miscellaneous items and service line (milk cooler). Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025
The hand-washing sink in the staff restroom was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Food contact and non-food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, principal's popcorn machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The door to the outside, located near the dry storage room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings under the pan storage area and the old tray return line. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025
The following were not in good repair: -inside lid to chest freezer -gaskets to small milk cooler -breakfast cooler not maintaining temperature (49 degrees). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Large milk coolers were observed with ambient temperatures of 44 and 50 degrees. Milks were moved to other refrigeration during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Both large milk coolers were observed in poor repair, not maintaining temperatures at or below 41 degrees. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
Chest freezer located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, observed with a crack in the inside lid. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Deli Meat, cheeses, and other foods were observed held at 49-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Potentially hazardous foods that have been out of temperature for an unknown amount of time was voluntarily disposed of. Foods that were recently delivered were moved to other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Thermometer is lacking in the 1 door refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

1 door refrigerator was observed at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Refrigerator was taken out of service during time of inspection. No TCS/PHF foods to be stored in refrigerator until repaired/ adjusted/ replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

Cooked chicken drumsticks were observed held at 109-125 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

Beans were observed held at 106 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

Buffalo Chicken Salad, Cold Bento Boxes were observed held at 63 and 55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was voluntarily disposed of by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

Milk was observed held at 47-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk was transported to other school to be blast chilled. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

Salad bar items (lettuce, zucchini, cucumber) were observed held at 47-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Salad bar removed from lunch during time of inspection. PIC decided to dispose of foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023
Milk cooler gaskets and door was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
